summary: All spec requirements are fully implemented and all 12 tests pass (5 new + 7 pre-existing). 3-way merge logic, conflict handling, .base.md filtering, audit log actions, and two-pass atomicity all match spec exactly.

checklist_results.note: merge_3way() (create_change_merge.rs lines 267–300) invokes `git merge-file --stdout` on ours/base/theirs temp files, returns Ok on exit 0 and Err with conflict count on exit >0. find_git_binary() (lines 305–318) falls back to overwrite-with-warning when git is absent. collect_spec_paths_into() skips .base.md files (helpers.rs lines 691–695). Two-pass atomicity (validation+merge pass lines 102–189, write pass lines 200–213) and audit log with create/overwrite/3way-merge actions (line 206) are all correct. 3-way merge condition requires both base_path.exists() && target_path.exists() (line 143) — semantically correct and a conservative safe extension of the spec.

checklist_results.item: [HARD] Spec has no ## Test Plan section — hard test-presence check N/A
checklist_results.note: The spec (merge-3way.md) has no ## Test Plan section. Hard reject rule does not apply. However, the ## Changes section (lines 253–283) specified 5 required test functions — all are present.

All 12 tests in create_change_merge::tests pass: the 5 new 3-way merge tests (test_3way_merge_clean, test_3way_merge_conflict, test_base_md_skipped_by_find_specs, test_no_base_fallback_overwrite, test_audit_log_3way_merge) plus 7 pre-existing tests. `cargo test -p cclab-sdd create_change_merge` reports 12 passed, 0 failed.

soft_issues.description: find_git_binary() (create_change_merge.rs lines 305–318) uses `which git` subprocess — Unix-only. On Windows, `where git` is needed. Low severity since cclab targets Unix/macOS.

The spec flowchart CheckBase node branches solely on .base.md existence. The implementation additionally requires target_path.exists() (line 143) to enter 3-way merge path — semantically correct (cannot 3-way merge without an 'ours'), but the guard is undocumented in the spec. No functional issue.
